titleStmt.title: Letter from James Davidson to Clara Davidson, 16 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Davidson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from James Davidson (1877-1916) to his mother, Clara Davidson, apologising for his delay in writing. Davidson has been settling back in with his company, who are in reserve at the French front line. Davidson writes that leave has been stopped and those on leave have been recalled suggesting that something serious is about to happen.This letter is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
16/4/16 My dearest Mother Have not
written you since Wed last, though
indeed there has not been much to
write about, but I have been awfully
busy getting a grip of things again
& tightening up the strings. It's
extraordinary how soon slackness
can creep in. We are back from
the lines where I found the Coy upon
arrival but I think it won't be long
before we are back again. Meantime
we have to keep up training, besides
doing all kinds of fatigues in
cleaning up roads & c. maybe 'etc' . Weather
has been so cold & stormy â day
before yest it actually snowed
but today there was a considerable
improvement & hope it will
continue. Thanks dear Mother
for your letter of 13th & also for
the parcels from Fureys &
Malcolmsons which arrived today.
Did I tell you in my last letter
that leave had been stopped again
& all men on leave have been
recalled. It rather points to something
serious but of course one never
knows. Dermot Neill had such
bad luck. He was actually at
the station when the order came
canceling the leave. Was so
glad to hear that Mrs Rogers is
making a good recovery.
With fondest love for all your loving son Jim
Active Service
Recd20thApril /16 Mrs. S. C. Davidson Seacourt Bangor Co Down. Ireland Js Davidson
16/4/16
